toc_primary.toc_indicator: Number of country-led or regionally-led demand articulation processes supported by CGIAR that result in prioritization of scaling-relevant problem–solution bundles
toc_primary.toc_result_title: I-OC 1.1: CGIAR’s alignment with regional and national priorities is clarified and increased
toc_primary.toc_result_description: CGIAR and NARES scientists collaboratively assess and prioritize stakeholder demands to ensure that agricultural innovations address the most pressing and context-specific needs. This helps shift CGIAR’s approach from a predominantly research-supply model to a demand-driven one, where local actors are active participants in shaping the research and scaling agenda.

assessment_type: Only Current innovation readiness and innovation use were self-assessed by the workshop experts

core_innovation.title: Drought Management System for environmental and agricultural policy makers to design and implement more science-based, integrated, and practical drought interventions benefiting climate vulnerable rural communities

bilateral_projects.project_title: T-PJ-003772-TAAT Clearinghouse: Re-invest to Accelerate Innovation Adoption

result_description: The Zambia Drought Management System incorporates multi-source information, namely access to real-time weather updates and open-access satellite data, that provides farmers, extension workers, agriculture and water resources authorities with all the information needed to forecast, monitor and manage drought. Specifically, it provides seasonal, sub-seasonal and seven-day weather forecasts to indicate when drought is present. The system incorporates national- to regional-level datasets drawn from multiple satellites, observed data and other sources. Therefore, the ZADMS can guide national, state and district-level authorities in the country to take timely action to prepare for drought. This has helped to cushion smallholders, many of them poor, from the worst effects of a prolonged drought situation.
